Should we do two conversions here, or would it make sense to create a new conversion method from the cm handle data node, to an ncmp service cm handle?

for this and below, you also try can add line breaks so easier to read (https://www.baeldung.com/swagger-format-descriptions#1-line-breaks-and-paragraphs) i.e.

description: |
  Execute cm handle query search, to be included in the result a cm-handle must fulfill ALL the conditions listed here, if one of the given module names does not exists, return with an empty collection.
  By supplying a CPS Path it is possible to query on any data related to the cm handle.
  
  For more on CPS Path please refer to documentation https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-cps/en/latest/cps-path.html. The cm handle ancestor is automatically returned for this query.

Changed descriptions to be unique in what they return, otherwise they do carry out the same function. I was also advised to make clear the use of cps path, is very long but is also a form of documentation so I think its ok

Would move this beneath the methods that reference it.

What is the difference between null(the default value of the map) and empty here with this map? How can it be null in one situation, and empty in another? Could we not just initialize it as an empty map, and then it makes the null check irrelevant.

I think its the for loop that makes the difference for it, so for the first loop it performs a call to get all the CMHandles for the first properties pair and then for every pair after that it just removes the CMHandles from the original list that aren't there for that public properties pair, more efficient that way rather than querying for all the properties and then having large lists of CMHandles for each property query pair. If the list is empty it can finish because its not going to add more cmhandles to the list

If it is done the way I mentioned above, all the tests still pass, I also did some manual tests around it and it seems to work. I think maybe the tests don't cover enough scenarios if you don't think this should work?
Yes but I forgot to add it! Will do now thanks!
Its to do with efficiency, we don't want to convert cmhandles on every iteration of the loop because it is expensive, so we convert once and then remove from the list using the ids
I did have to add it, I don't mind where it goes so can move if team agree 😊
queryDataNodes from InventoryPersistence is renamed to this method.

Kind of, queryDatanodes in InventoryPersistence was only being used to query cmhandles and the /ancestor::cm-handles was being appended everytime, so method has been renamed and the ancestor::cm-handles is appended in just this method instead of in all the methods which call this method. Myself and Toine decided on it :)
